Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing In Support Of Request For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day, having been continued from Monday, April 18, 2022 for further Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendants Derek Chu, an individual, Karen Chu, an individual, DCHU Holdings, LLC, a California limited liability company and Suitelife NORCAL LLC, a California limited liability company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:00 a.m.

Jeffrey A. Feldman, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Jonathan Mayeda, individually, and as Successor Trustee to the Satoshi and Betty Mayeda 2012 Revocable Living Trust, who was placed under oath and testified.
Carol Meyeda, formerly a plaintiff in this matter, was placed under oath and testified.*
Melinda Hohnhauser, Mr. Feldman's assistant and witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Messrs. Feldman and Mayeda, along with Ms. Meyda and Ms. Hohnhauser, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Derek Chu, an individual, Karen Chu, an individual, DCHU Holdings, LLC, a California limited liability company or Suitelife NORCAL LLC, a California limited liability company.

Plaintiffs' Complaint for Breach of Contract, Fraud, Conversion, et al., was filed on February 8, 2019.
Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint for Breach of Contract, Fraud, Constructive Fraud, Breach of Fiduciary Duty, Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage, Aiding and Abetting, Violation of Corporations Code Sections 25401, et seq. and 25504.1, et seq., Negligent Supervision and Common Counts was filed on August 6, 2019.
Default as to defendants Derik Chu and Suitelife NORCAL, LLC were entered on December 17, 2019.
Default as to defendant DCHU Holdings, LLC was entered on October 11, 2019.
Default as to defendant Karen Chu was entered on May 7, 2021.
Dismissal as to defendants New York Life Insurance Company, NYLIFE Securities LLC and Felix Chu was filed on November 12, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on May 10, 2019.
Dismissal of the First Amended Complaint as to plaintiff Carol Mayeda, only, was filed with the Clerk's Office on April 18, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Jonathan Mayeda, individually, and as Successor Trustee to the Satoshi and Betty Mayeda 2012 Revocable Living Trust, and against the defaulted defendants Derek Chu, an individual, Karen Chu, an individual, DCHU Holdings, LLC, a California limited liability company and Suitelife NORCAL LLC, a California limited liability company, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $800,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - WAIVED.
Attorney Fees - 266,667.00.
Costs - $2,823.83.

Total - $1,069,490.83.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 11:38 a.m.

*Plaintiff Carol Meyda was DISMISSED from this matter at the last calling of the case. At that time the Court Voir Dired Ms. Meyda as to her understanding of the ramifications of voluntarily dismissing from this matter.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Allegations In The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Feldman, Esq. 415-391-5555 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Andrew Azarmi, Esq. 415-356-4631 of Dentons US LLP for Defendant NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NYLIFE SECURITIES, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues a narrower issue re: Derek Chu and the note. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NYLIFE SECURITIES, LLC's Motion To Strike Allegations In The First Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants New York Life Insurance Company and NYLIFE Securities, LLC's motion to strike is denied. The adduced allegations are not "irrelevant, false, or improper" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, 436(a). Even if plaintiffs cannot establish respondeat superior liability based on Defendant Derek Chu's conduct, the allegations are proper to support other theories of liability such as aiding and abetting. The allegations are also proper as to other defendants.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Feldman, Esq. 415-391-5555 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Andrew Azarmi, Esq. 415-356-4631 of Dentons US LLP for Defendant NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NYLIFE SECURITIES, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues a narrower issue re: Derek Chu and the note. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NYLIFE SECURITIES, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, NYLIFE SECURITIES, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective advantage. (See Applied Equip. Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 513-514; PM Group, Inc. v. Stewart (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 55, 64-65 ["The tort of intentional interference with contractual relations is committed only by strangers-interlopers who have no legitimate interest in the scope or course of the contract's performance" (internal quotes omitted).)

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
